title: Do not architect around the removed query cache
description: MySQL 8 deleted the query cache entirely, so designs that lean on it must move caching to the application layer.
body: Use when porting a MySQL 5.x design to 8.x or reviewing code that assumes server-side result caching. Do not set or tune query_cache_size or expect repeated identical SELECTs to be served from a built-in cache; the feature was removed in MySQL 8 because its global mutex serialised writes. Actions: remove query_cache settings from my.cnf, move read caching to an explicit application or Redis layer with clear invalidation, and verify the server starts without unknown-variable warnings. Validation signals include the server log showing no query_cache references and a benchmark showing the app cache absorbing repeated reads. Risk boundary: leaning on the cache for correctness rather than as an optimisation is a design smell, so flag any code that assumes cached freshness.
